subtopics: Identify and use natural numbers, integers, prime numbers, square numbers, cube numbers, common factors, common multiples, rational and irrational numbers, and reciprocals
Express numbers as products of prime factors; find HCF and LCM of two numbers
Understand and use set language, notation and Venn diagrams (two or three sets) including n(A), complement, universal set, subset, union, intersection, empty set
Calculate with squares, square roots, cubes, cube roots, and other powers and roots (recall squares 1-15, cubes of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 10)
Use proper fractions, improper fractions, mixed numbers, decimals and percentages; convert between forms including recurring decimals
Convert recurring decimals to fractions and vice versa
Order quantities using =, ≠, >, <, ≥ and ≤
Use the four operations with integers, fractions and decimals including negative numbers, improper fractions, mixed numbers and correct ordering of operations
Understand and use indices (positive, zero, negative and fractional) and the rules of indices
Use standard form A × 10^n; convert into and out of standard form; calculate with standard form
Round to specified accuracy (decimal places, significant figures); make estimates; round answers appropriately
Give upper and lower bounds for data rounded to specified accuracy; find bounds of results of calculations using rounded data
Understand and use ratio and proportion: simplify ratios, divide a quantity in a given ratio, use proportional reasoning in context
Use common measures of rate (pay, exchange rates, flow rates, fuel consumption) and other measures (pressure, density, population density); solve average speed problems
Calculate percentages of a quantity, express one quantity as a percentage of another, calculate percentage increase/decrease
Calculate with simple and compound interest; calculate using reverse percentages; solve repeated percentage change problems
Use a calculator efficiently; enter values appropriately; interpret calculator displays (money, time)
Calculate with time (s, min, h, days, weeks, months, years); 24-hour and 12-hour clock; read clocks and timetables; solve time zone problems
Calculate with money; convert between currencies
Use exponential growth and decay (depreciation, population change)
Understand and use surds including simplifying expressions and rationalising the denominator
topics.description: Extended candidates work with natural numbers, integers, primes, squares, cubes, surds, sets, fractions, decimals, percentages, indices, standard form, estimation, bounds, ratio, proportion, rates, percentages, compound interest, reverse percentages, exponential growth/decay, calculator skills, time and money calculations.

subtopics: Use letters to represent generalised numbers; substitute numbers into expressions and formulas
Simplify expressions by collecting like terms; expand products of algebraic expressions including products of two or more brackets
Factorise by extracting common factors; factorise ax + bx + kay + kby, a²x² − b²y², a² + 2ab + b², ax² + bx + c, ax³ + bx² + cx
Complete the square for expressions in the form ax² + bx + c
Manipulate algebraic fractions; factorise and simplify rational expressions
Understand and use indices (positive, zero, negative and fractional) with algebraic terms; solve index equations
Construct expressions, equations and formulas; solve linear equations in one unknown
Solve fractional equations with numerical and linear algebraic denominators
Solve simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns; solve simultaneous equations with one linear and one non-linear (up to power 2)
Solve quadratic equations by factorisation, completing the square and quadratic formula; give solutions in surd form
Change the subject of formulas where subject appears twice or involves powers/roots
Represent and interpret inequalities on a number line; construct, solve and interpret linear inequalities
Represent linear inequalities in two variables graphically; list inequalities defining a given region
Continue sequences; recognise patterns including term-to-term rule; find and use nth term of linear, quadratic, cubic and exponential sequences
Express direct and inverse proportion in algebraic terms (linear, square, square root, cube, cube root); use proportionality symbol
Use and interpret travel graphs and conversion graphs; draw graphs from given data
Apply rate of change to kinematics: distance-time and speed-time graphs, acceleration/deceleration; calculate distance as area under speed-time graph
Construct tables of values and draw, recognise, interpret graphs of functions: ax^n, ab^x + c for various n including negative and fractional
Solve associated equations graphically; interpret roots; draw exponential growth/decay graphs
Recognise, sketch and interpret linear, quadratic, cubic, reciprocal and exponential graphs; knowledge of turning points, roots, symmetry, asymptotes
Estimate gradients of curves by drawing tangents; differentiate ax^n and simple sums; find stationary points; discriminate between maxima and minima
Understand functions, domain and range, function notation; find inverse functions; form composite functions gf(x) = g(f(x))
topics.description: Extended candidates manipulate algebraic expressions including factorising quadratics and completing the square, solve linear, fractional, simultaneous and quadratic equations, work with inequalities including graphical regions, use sequences, direct and inverse proportion, interpret and draw graphs of various functions including exponential and reciprocal, apply kinematic concepts, use differentiation to find gradients and turning points, and work with functions including composite and inverse functions.

subtopics: Use and interpret Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions
Draw straight-line graphs for linear equations in form y = mx + c and ax + by = c
Find the gradient of a straight line from a grid and from coordinates of two points
Calculate the length of a line segment using coordinates
Find the coordinates of the midpoint of a line segment
Interpret and obtain the equation of a straight-line graph in forms y = mx + c, ax + by = c, x = k
Find the gradient and equation of a straight line parallel to a given line
Find the gradient and equation of a straight line perpendicular to a given line
Find the equation of the perpendicular bisector of a line joining two points
topics.description: Extended candidates use Cartesian coordinates, draw and interpret linear graphs, calculate gradient, length and midpoint of line segments, find equations of straight lines in various forms, and work with parallel and perpendicular lines including perpendicular bisectors.

subtopics: Use and interpret geometrical terms: point, vertex, line, plane, parallel, perpendicular, perpendicular bisector, bearing, right angle, acute, obtuse, reflex angles
Use vocabulary of triangles (equilateral, isosceles, scalene, right-angled), quadrilaterals (square, rectangle, kite, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium), polygons and solids
Use vocabulary of circles: centre, radius, diameter, circumference, semicircle, chord, tangent, arc, major/minor arc, sector, segment
Use vocabulary of solids: cube, cuboid, prism, cylinder, pyramid, cone, sphere, hemisphere, frustum, face, surface, edge
Measure and draw lines and angles; construct triangles using ruler and compasses; draw and interpret nets
Draw and interpret scale drawings; use and interpret three-figure bearings (000° to 360°)
Calculate lengths of similar shapes; use relationships between lengths, areas and volumes of similar shapes and solids
Solve problems involving similarity; show triangles are similar using geometric reasons
Recognise line symmetry and order of rotational symmetry in 2D; recognise symmetry properties of prisms, cylinders, pyramids and cones
Calculate unknown angles using angle properties at a point, on a straight line, vertically opposite, triangle and quadrilateral angle sums
Calculate angles in parallel lines: corresponding, alternate, co-interior angles
Know and use angle properties of regular and irregular polygons including exterior and interior angles
Use circle theorems: angle in semicircle, angle between tangent and radius, angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference, angles in same segment, cyclic quadrilateral, alternate segment theorem
Use symmetry properties of circles: equal chords equidistant from centre, perpendicular bisector of chord through centre, tangents from external point equal
topics.description: Extended candidates use and interpret geometrical terms for angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, circles and solids including hemispheres and frustums, construct triangles and nets, work with scale drawings and bearings, solve similarity problems including area and volume ratios, calculate angles using properties of parallel lines and polygons, and apply full circle theorems and symmetry properties of circles.

subtopics: Use metric units of mass, length, area, volume and capacity; convert between units including area (cm² ↔ m²) and volume/capacity (m³ ↔ litres)
Calculate perimeter and area of rectangle, triangle, parallelogram and trapezium
Calculate circumference and area of a circle; calculate arc length and sector area as fractions of circumference and area (including minor and major sectors)
Calculate surface area and volume of cuboid, prism, cylinder, sphere, pyramid and cone; use given formulas for curved surface areas and volumes
Solve problems involving perimeters and areas of compound shapes and parts of shapes
Solve problems involving surface areas and volumes of compound solids and parts of solids including frustums
topics.description: Extended candidates use and convert metric units, calculate perimeter and area of common shapes, work with circles including arc lengths and sector areas (major and minor), calculate surface area and volume of prisms, cylinders, spheres, pyramids and cones, and solve problems involving compound shapes and parts of solids including frustums.

subtopics: Know and use Pythagoras' theorem
Know and use sine, cosine and tangent ratios for acute angles in right-angled triangles
Solve problems in two dimensions using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry
Know that perpendicular distance from a point to a line is the shortest distance
Carry out calculations involving angles of elevation and depression
Know exact values of sin x and cos x for x = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90° and tan x for x = 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°
Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of y = sin x, y = cos x, y = tan x for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°
Solve trigonometric equations involving sin x, cos x or tan x for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°
Use the sine rule and cosine rule in calculations for any triangle including obtuse angles and the ambiguous case
Use formula area of triangle = (1/2)ab sin C
Solve problems in three dimensions using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry including angle between a line and a plane
topics.description: Extended candidates use Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometric ratios in right-angled triangles, solve 2D problems including angles of elevation and depression, know exact trigonometric values, sketch and interpret sin, cos and tan graphs and solve trig equations for 0° to 360°, apply sine and cosine rules including the ambiguous case, calculate area using (1/2)ab sin C, and solve 3D problems including angles between lines and planes.

subtopics: Recognise, describe and draw reflections in a straight line
Recognise, describe and draw rotations about a centre through multiples of 90°
Recognise, describe and draw enlargements from a centre by positive, fractional and negative scale factors
Recognise, describe and draw translations by a vector
Solve problems involving combinations of transformations
Describe translations using vector notation; add and subtract vectors; multiply a vector by a scalar
Calculate the magnitude of a vector x/y as √(x² + y²)
Represent vectors by directed line segments; use position vectors
Use sum and difference of vectors to express given vectors in terms of two coplanar vectors
Use vectors to reason and solve geometric problems: show vectors are parallel, show 3 points are collinear, solve ratio and similarity problems
topics.description: Extended candidates recognise, describe and draw all four transformations (reflection, rotation, enlargement, translation) including combinations and negative scale factors, work with vectors in two dimensions including addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication and magnitude, use position vectors, and apply vector methods to solve geometric problems involving parallelism, collinearity, ratio and similarity.

subtopics: Understand and use the probability scale from 0 to 1; use probability notation P(A), P(A')
Calculate the probability of a single event; understand P(not A) = 1 − P(A)
Understand relative frequency as an estimate of probability; calculate expected frequencies
Understand fair, bias and random in probability contexts
Calculate probability of combined events using sample space diagrams, Venn diagrams and tree diagrams
Work with combined events with or without replacement
Use Venn diagram notation P(A ∩ B) and P(A ∪ B)
Calculate conditional probability using Venn diagrams, tree diagrams and tables
topics.description: Extended candidates understand and use probability notation and scale, calculate probabilities of single and combined events with or without replacement using sample space diagrams, Venn diagrams (including notation for intersection and union) and tree diagrams, understand relative frequency and expected frequencies, and calculate conditional probability.

subtopics: Classify and tabulate statistical data using tally tables and two-way tables
Read, interpret and draw inferences from tables and statistical diagrams; compare data sets
Appreciate restrictions on drawing conclusions from given data
Calculate mean, median, mode, quartiles, range and interquartile range for individual data
Calculate an estimate of the mean for grouped discrete or grouped continuous data
Identify the modal class from a grouped frequency distribution
Draw and interpret bar charts, pie charts, pictograms, stem-and-leaf diagrams and simple frequency distributions
Draw and interpret composite and dual bar charts; ordered stem-and-leaf diagrams with key
Draw and interpret scatter diagrams; understand positive, negative and zero correlation
Draw by eye and use a straight line of best fit on scatter diagrams
Draw and interpret cumulative frequency tables and diagrams; estimate median, percentiles, quartiles and interquartile range
Draw and interpret histograms; calculate with frequency density
topics.description: Extended candidates classify and tabulate data, interpret and compare statistical diagrams, calculate measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and spread (range, quartiles, interquartile range) for individual and grouped data, draw and interpret various charts including cumulative frequency diagrams and histograms, and work with scatter diagrams and lines of best fit.
